Title: xCart — per-line validationErrors non-empty after over-stock changeQty (VCST-5234 regression guard — PASS after PR #124; line-level code is PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED/LineItem)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Uses BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY (Animal Crossing). Repro shape: add qty=1 (succeeds), then changeCartItemQuantity to qty=1568 (over-stock). LINE_ITEM_ID captured via follow-up cart query (addItem.items[] is empty due to async cart-projection settle — see reference_additem_async_settle memory). The final read_back query intentionally omits cart-level validationErrors to avoid triggering CartType.validationErrors resolver which re-validates and refills CartValidationErrors, masking the bug. REGRESSION GUARD for VCST-5234: ClearValidationCache() in SaveAsync() wipes CartValidationErrors so LineItemType resolvers return isValid=true / validationErrors=[] even on an invalid line. EXPECTED TO FAIL on XCart <=3.1018.0-pr-120 until fix ships. NOTE: If changeCartItemQuantity returns itemsCount=0 the FFC gate rejected the over-stock change and per-line assertions will not probe the VCST-5234 code path in this environment (valid skip condition). The per-line FAIL is only meaningful when the line IS retained at the over-stock qty.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=get_line_id] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_line_id] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_to_overstock] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID}}].quantity = 1568
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID}}].isValid = false
[COUNT label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID}}].validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID}}].validationErrors[?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].errorCode = PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] VCST-5234 regression guard — this case MUST FAIL on XCart <=3.1018.0-pr-120; PASS after fix. cart-level validationErrors intentionally omitted from read_back to isolate per-line resolver behaviour (CartType.validationErrors triggers re-validation which refills CartValidationErrors; omitting it ensures LineItemType resolvers are reading from cleared sync store — the true buggy state).
Failure_Signals: isValid=true after over-stock changeQty (PASS on broken build = silent failure); validationErrors=[] on over-stock line; LINE_ITEM_ID null capture (check get_line_id succeeded and addItem included currencyCode+cultureName); itemsCount=0 after add_item (missing currencyCode/cultureName in addItem command)

Title: xCart — cart-level validationErrors(ruleSet:default) stays clean of item-qty errors after over-stock (qty errors are items-scope, not default)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Uses BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY (availableQuantity=568). Verifies that the CartType.validationErrors resolver re-validates on each read (the working path that PR #110 wired up). add qty=1, capture LINE_ITEM_ID via follow-up cart query, changeCartItemQuantity to 1568, then query cart with validationErrors(ruleSet:"default") — verifies item-qty errors (PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED/PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT) do NOT appear in the default scope (they live in the items ruleSet; default stays clean). Live-verified 2026-06-11. LINE_ITEM_ID captured via cart query, not addItem response (async settle).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=get_line_id] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_line_id] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_to_overstock] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_default_ruleset] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_default_ruleset] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[COUNT label=read_default_ruleset] data.cart.validationErrors[*?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].length = 0
[COUNT label=read_default_ruleset] data.cart.validationErrors[*?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT].length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Live-verified 2026-06-11: item-qty errors (PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED/PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT) live in the items ruleSet, NOT default; the default scope stays clean of them after over-stock
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on change_to_overstock; validationErrors empty on over-stocked cart (re-validation broken); PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT absent; itemsCount=0 after add_item (missing currencyCode/cultureName)

Create over-stock state, then correct it by reducing qty to 1 (within stock), then verify cart.validationErrors(ruleSet:default) is empty AND per-line isValid=true / validationErrors=[]. Guards the stale-error scenario that ClearValidationCache was meant to fix. LINE_ITEM_ID via follow-up cart query (addItem async settle).

Title: xCart — cart-level validationErrors(ruleSet:items) surfaces the over-stock error PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED after changeQty (ruleSet used by PR #124 LineItemType fix)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. Tests the ruleSet:"items" path specifically — this is the exact ruleSet that XCart PR #124 (fix for VCST-5234) uses inside LineItemType to re-validate line items. If cart-level validationErrors(ruleSet:"items") does not return PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT, the PR #124 fix cannot re-validate per-line items correctly. add qty=1, capture LINE_ITEM_ID via follow-up cart query (addItem.items[] async settle), changeCartItemQuantity to 1568 (over-stock), then query cart.validationErrors(ruleSet:"items") — must contain PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT. REGRESSION GUARD for VCST-5234 PR #124 architectural seam.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=get_line_id] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_line_id] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_to_overstock] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_items_ruleset] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_items_ruleset] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[COUNT label=read_items_ruleset] data.cart.validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=read_items_ruleset] data.cart.validationErrors[?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].errorCode = PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] ruleSet:"items" is the exact ruleSet XCart PR #124 passes to CartAggregate.ValidateAsync() inside LineItemType resolver. The over-stock condition surfaces here as PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED / LineItem (live-verified 2026-06-11). If this ruleSet returns [] on an over-stocked cart, the per-line re-validation introduced by PR #124 is broken at the architectural level — even if ruleSet:default behaves.
Failure_Signals: read_items_ruleset returns validationErrors=[] on an over-stocked cart (ruleSet:items not populating errors); PRODUCT_QTY_INSUFFICIENT absent from ruleSet:items result; LINE_ITEM_ID null capture (check get_line_id and addItem currencyCode+cultureName); itemsCount=0 after add_item (missing currencyCode/cultureName in addItem command)

Title: xCart — per-line isValid isolation: over-stock line does not contaminate sibling valid line (live-discovered B2B products)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. LIVE-DISCOVERY — no product aliases (the PROD_* standard-product aliases are NOT linked into the active B2B virtual catalog and silently fail to add; only catalog-resident products add). Discovers two distinct in-stock products from the B2B virtual catalog via products(filter: category.subtree:@td(VIRTUAL_CATALOG_B2B.id) availability:InStock): items[0] = VALID sibling (qty 1, stays valid), items[1] = over-stock line (changeQty to OVER_STOCK_QTY). The B2B catalog is dominated by inventory-tracked products (verified 2026-06-11: 4498 in-stock, 14/15 first-page items tracked+buyable), so items[1] is virtually always tracked; if a rare untracked product is picked the isValid=false assertion fails LOUDLY (re-run, not a false-pass). Line-scope over-stock error = PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED/LineItem. cartName+currencyCode+cultureName REQUIRED on every cart op or it resolves a different/empty cart. LINE_ITEM IDs captured via follow-up cart query (addItem async settle).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=discover] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=discover] data.products.items.length >= 2
[ERRORS label=add_a] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_a] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=add_b] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_b] data.addItem.itemsCount = 2
[ERRORS label=get_line_ids] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_line_ids] data.cart.itemsCount = 2
[ERRORS label=change_b_overstock] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsCount = 2
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID_B}}].isValid = false
[COUNT label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID_B}}].validationErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID_B}}].validationErrors[?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].errorCode = PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID_A}}].isValid = true
[COUNT label=read_back] data.cart.items[?id={{LINE_ITEM_ID_A}}].validationErrors.length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Multi-line isolation: the over-stock line (LINE_ITEM_ID_B) is invalid; the sibling in-stock line (LINE_ITEM_ID_A, qty 1) must remain isValid=true with no errors. A fault in VCST-5234 or a future regression could mark ALL lines invalid when any one line fails — this case detects that. Products live-discovered from the B2B virtual catalog (no fixed aliases).
Failure_Signals: discover returns <2 items (catalog empty or B2B subtree filter wrong); itemsCount!=2 after add_a+add_b (a discovered product is non-buyable, or same-productId consolidation — items[0]/items[1] are distinct catalog entries so consolidation should not occur); LINE_ITEM_ID_A or _B null (capture predicate failed / product not in cart); LINE_ITEM_ID_A.isValid=false (contamination bug — invalid sibling poisoning valid line); LINE_ITEM_ID_B.isValid=true after over-stock (pre-fix regression, OR a rare untracked product was discovered at items[1] — re-run to confirm)

Title: xCart — within-request ruleSet cache isolation: ruleSet:* resolved first does NOT leak its errors into ruleSet:shipments in the same request (aliased fields)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. VCST-4952 PR #110 review Test 1: within a SINGLE cart() query, request both ruleSet:"*" and ruleSet:"shipments" via GraphQL aliases (allErrors / shippingErrors). Before the PR #110 caching fix, ruleSet:"*" sealed the shared cache as IsValidated=true, causing ruleSet:"shipments" in the same request to echo the full error list instead of re-validating for its own scope. Expected: allErrors (ruleSet:*) contains PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED (over-stock line error live-verified on XCart 3.1019.0-pr-124 / 2026-06-11); shippingErrors (ruleSet:shipments) does NOT contain PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED (item qty errors are not shipment-scope errors). Tests the within-request cache isolation that PR #110 introduced. BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY qty=1568, stock=568 → confirmed over-stock; LINE_ITEM_ID via follow-up cart query.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_item] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_item] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=get_line_id] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_line_id] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=change_to_overstock] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=read_aliased] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_aliased] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[COUNT label=read_aliased] data.cart.allErrors.length >= 1
[DATA label=read_aliased] data.cart.allErrors[?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].errorCode = PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED
[COUNT label=read_aliased] data.cart.shippingErrors[*?errorCode=PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED].length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Within-request alias isolation (VCST-4952 Test 1). allErrors (ruleSet:*) must contain PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED for the over-stock line; shippingErrors (ruleSet:shipments) must NOT contain PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED. A cache-sealed-by-* regression would return PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED in both alias fields in the same response.
Failure_Signals: shippingErrors contains PRODUCT_QTY_CHANGED (ruleSet bleed from * into shipments — the pre-PR#110 regression); allErrors empty on an over-stocked cart (wildcard not populating errors); LINE_ITEM_ID null capture; itemsCount=0 after add_item (missing cartName/currencyCode/cultureName)

Before the PR #110 caching fix, ruleSet:"foo" ran first, wrote [] into the shared cache under IsValidated=true, and then ruleSet:"*" returned [] for an actually-invalid cart — silent false-clean result. Title: xCart/Skyflow — saved-card list (skyflowCards) returns masked PAN + skyflowId with NO raw PAN, and deleteSkyflowCard removes a disposable card (API surface for saved-card delete)
Preconditions: Runner-native. USER_DEFAULT authenticated (saved Skyflow cards live on the storefront user, not the org). Schema surface confirmed via live introspection 2026-06-12: query skyflowCards(storeId:String) -> SkyflowCardResponseType { cards { skyflowId cardNumber cardType cardScheme cardholderName expiryMonth expiryYear active } } (cardNumber is the MASKED PAN — vault never returns raw PAN to the client); mutation deleteSkyflowCard(command: DeleteSkyflowCardCommandType { skyflowId:String! storeId:String! }) -> Boolean. PRECONDITION: USER_DEFAULT must hold >=2 saved Skyflow cards — the canonical Mastercard ending 0015 (SKYFLOW_MC, seeded by PAY-SKY-009) PLUS at least one DISPOSABLE card to delete (seed a 2nd via a new-card cart payment with Save card checked, e.g. PAY-SKY-018). The case deletes cards[1] (the 2nd / disposable card), NEVER cards[0], so the canonical MC used by the success cases is preserved. If skyflowCards returns <2 cards the delete cannot run deterministically — that is a seed-state gap; re-seed a disposable card, do NOT delete the canonical MC. REGRESSION GUARD that the saved-card API surface (list + delete) exists and never leaks raw PAN — guards VCST-5009 / PR #2328 saved-card mechanics at the API layer.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=list_cards_before] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=list_cards_before] data.skyflowCards.cards.length >= 2
[DATA label=list_cards_before] data.skyflowCards.cards[0].skyflowId is non-null
[DATA label=list_cards_before] data.skyflowCards.cards[0].cardNumber is non-null
[ERRORS label=delete_card] errors[] empty
[DATA label=delete_card] data.deleteSkyflowCard = true
[ERRORS label=list_cards_after] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=list_cards_after] data.skyflowCards.cards[*?skyflowId={{DISPOSABLE_SKYFLOW_ID}}].length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] API surface for saved-card list + delete (introspection-confirmed 2026-06-12). cards[].cardNumber is the masked PAN (e.g. XXXX0015) — assert it is non-null and present; the raw 16-digit PAN must NEVER appear in any response (vault tokenization, BL-PAY-001). Delete targets cards[1] (disposable) so the canonical MC (cards[0], ending {{CANONICAL_MASK_TAIL}}) is preserved for the success cases. deleteSkyflowCard returns a bare Boolean true. After delete, the disposable skyflowId is absent from skyflowCards.cards[].
Failure_Signals: skyflowCards query unresolved / errors[] non-empty (no API surface — STOP and report); cards[].cardNumber contains a full 16-digit PAN (raw-PAN leak, BL-PAY-001 violation — file P0 bug); deleteSkyflowCard returns false or errors[]; deleted skyflowId still present in list_cards_after (delete no-op); DISPOSABLE_SKYFLOW_ID null capture = fewer than 2 saved cards (seed gap — re-seed a disposable card, never delete the canonical MC)
Cleanup: Deletes the disposable card (cards[1]) as part of the flow; canonical MC preserved. To restore the disposable card, re-run PAY-SKY-018 (save-card persistence).
References: VCST-5009; vc-frontend PR#2328; Skyflow module 3.1003.0; schema introspection 2026-06-12 (skyflowCards/SkyflowCardResponseType, deleteSkyflowCard/DeleteSkyflowCardCommandType); BL-PAY-003, BL-PAY-001
